title: Household cleaning & surface disinfection (non-clinical)
context: Household surface cleaning and disinfection is a key hygiene promotion topic, particularly important during disease outbreaks. The non-clinical framing means this unit covers general household practice (cleaning methods, frequency, safe dilution guidance for common household disinfectants) without specifying clinical-grade concentrations or procedures. Any chemical dosing for water treatment is in the HIGH-risk M2 tasks. This unit builds on the authoring pattern from content-006.
objective: Author, source-cite, and get domain-reviewed a WashUnit document covering household cleaning and surface disinfection at the non-clinical household-practice level: what surfaces to clean and how often, general disinfection methods (soap/detergent cleaning, diluted household disinfectant use), and safe handling of cleaning products — without specifying clinical-grade dosing that belongs in HIGH-risk units.
acceptanceCriteria: Every claim tied to a specific named authoritative source (WHO/UNICEF/CDC) with retrieval date and verified license; no verbatim copied text; no logos/emblems; CI copy/similarity check passes
Unit conforms to the WashUnit schema: all required fields present, riskClass is medium, no clinical-grade dosing instruction, steps are plain-language per the style guide
Unit reviewed and approved by a WASH/public-health domain reviewer distinct from the author; a PR-tied review-log entry written; no brand or product recommendations included
CI schema validation, citation-coverage, no-self-approval, readability, and copy/similarity gates all pass
output: A committed, domain-reviewed, source-cited WashUnit document covering household cleaning and surface disinfection at the non-clinical level, with a PR-tied review-log entry.
